revision.comment: /* Color template */Fixed alphabetical error I made earlier.
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{Lua|Module:InfoboxImage|Module:WikidataIB|Module:Check for unknown parameters}} {{Tracks Wikidata|P18|cat=No local image but image on Wikidata}} The template can be used for any god, deity or mythological figure who is worshiped in a religion or cult. For other mythological figures, please consider other templates like [[Template:Infobox mythical creature]] or [[Template:Infobox character]]. == All fields == {{Parameter names example | type | name | deity_of | member_of | image | alt | caption | other_names | hiero | Old_Norse | script_name | script | affiliation | cult_center | abode | planet | mantra | weapon | battles | artifacts | animals | symbol | day | color | number | consort | parents | siblings | offspring | army | mount | texts | Greek_equivalent | Roman_equivalent | Etruscan_equivalent | Christian_equivalent | Slavic_equivalent | Hinduism_equivalent | Canaanite_equivalent| Indo-european_equivalent | equivalent1_type | equivalent1 | equivalent2_type | equivalent2 | equivalent3_type | equivalent3 | equivalent4_type | equivalent4 | equivalent5_type | equivalent5 | region | ethnic_group | festivals | height | age | tree | predecessor | successor | adherents | gender | venerated_in}} {{Infobox deity | type = | name = | deity_of = | member_of = | image = | alt = | caption = | other_names = | hiero = | avatar_birth = | avatar_end = | Old_Norse = | script_name = | script = | affiliation = | cult_center = | abode = | planet = | mantra = | mantra benefits = | weapon = | battles = | artifacts = | animals = | symbol = | adherents = | height = | age = | tree = | day = | color = | number = | consort = | parents = | siblings = | offspring = | predecessor = | successor = | army = | mount = | texts = | gender = | Greek_equivalent = | Roman_equivalent = | Etruscan_equivalent = | Christian_equivalent = | Islamic_equivalent = | Slavic_equivalent = | Hinduism_equivalent = | Canaanite_equivalent = | Indo-european_equivalent = | Maya_equivalent = | Aztec_equivalent = | equivalent1_type = | equivalent1 = | equivalent2_type = | equivalent2 = | equivalent3_type = | equivalent3 = | equivalent4_type = | equivalent4 = | equivalent5_type = | equivalent5 = | region = | ethnic_group = | festivals = | nirvana = }} {{clear}} ==Examples== {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[Mouse]] | symbols = [[Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[Mouse]] | symbols = [[Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{clear}} ==TemplateData== {{Collapse top|title=TemplateData}} {{TemplateData header|noheader=1}} { "description": "", "params": { "name": { "label": "Name", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "Character name to display in top header (default = PAGENAME)." }, "image": { "label": "Image", "type": "wiki-file-name", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "Image of the character. \"File:\" and wikilinking is not required. i.e. use {{para|image|Example.png}}." }, "image_upright": { "label": "Image upright", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "The image size defaults to frameless (220px). The size should not be set to a value that would result in an image width greater than 270px. See [[#Calculating \"image_upright\"|help below]] for guidance on determining the value to be used." }, "alt": { "label": "Alt", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "Alt text for image per [[WP:MOSALT]]." }, "caption": { "label": "Caption", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "A caption explaining the image." }, "type": { "label": "Type", "description": "Religion in which the deity is worshipped", "example": "Hindu for Rama", "type": "string", "required": true }, "avatar_birth" : {}, "avatar_end": {}, "deity_of": {}, "god_of": {}, "alusi_of": {}, "member_of": {}, "qid": {}, "suppressfields": {}, "fetchwikidata": {}, "image_size": {}, "other_names": {}, "hiero": {}, "old_norse": {}, "script_name": {}, "script": {}, "venerated_in": {}, "associate": {}, "affiliation": {}, "cult_centre": {}, "cult_center": {}, "predecessor": {}, "successor": {}, "abode": {}, "abodes": {}, "planet": {}, "world": {}, "mantra": {}, "weapon": {}, "weapons": {}, "army": {}, "battles": {}, "artefacts": {}, "artifacts": {}, "animals": {}, "symbol": {}, "symbols": {}, "adherents": {}, "height": {}, "age": {}, "tree": {}, "day": {}, "complexion": {}, "colour": {}, "color": {}, "number": {}, "mount": {}, "texts": {}, "gender": {}, "region": {}, "ethnic_group": {}, "temples": {}, "temple": {}, "festivals": {}, "birth_name": {}, "birth_date": {}, "birth_place": {}, "death_date": {}, "death_place": {}, "spouse": {}, "children": {}, "father": {}, "mother": {}, "siblings": {}, "dynasty": {}, "birthname": {}, "moksha_date": {}, "moksha_place": {}, "parents": {}, "consort": {}, "consorts": {}, "offspring": {}, "greek_equivalent": {}, "roman_equivalent": {}, "norse_equivalent": {}, "etruscan_equivalent": {}, "christian_equivalent": {}, "slavic_equivalent": {}, "hinduism_equivalent": {}, "canaanite_equivalent": {}, "Indo-european_equivalent": {}, "equivalent1": {}, "equivalent2": {}, "equivalent3": {}, "equivalent4": {}, "equivalent5": {}, "equivalent1_type": {}, "equivalent2_type": {}, "equivalent3_type": {}, "equivalent4_type": {}, "equivalent5_type": {}, "child": {}, "nocat_wdimage": {} }, "format": "{{_\n| _ = _\n}}" } {{Collapse bottom}} ==Color template == If “|type= “ is filled with any of the following inputs, it will use colors from the infobox drawn from [[Template:Infobox deity/color]] {| class="wikitable" |+Deity type and color style !|type = Input !Color style output !Sidebar matching? !Footer matching? |- |Akan |style="background-color:#FFD700;" | '''#FFD700''' |No |{{Tick}} [[Template:Akan topics|Yes]] |- |Arabian |style="background-color:#cdc8b8;" | '''#CDC8B8''' | | |- |Australian |style="background-color:#eba9ee"|'''#EBA9EE''' | | |- |Aztec |style="background-color:#00a86b; color: #FFFFFF;" | '''#00A86B''' | | |- |Bantu |style="background-color:#bdfdfb" | '''#BDFDFB''' | | |- revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{Lua|Module:InfoboxImage|Module:WikidataIB|Module:Check for unknown parameters}} {{Tracks Wikidata|P18|cat=No local image but image on Wikidata}} The template can be used for any god, deity or mythological figure who is worshiped in a religion or cult. For other mythological figures, please consider other templates like [[Template:Infobox mythical creature]] or [[Template:Infobox character]]. == All fields == {{Parameter names example | type | name | deity_of | member_of | image | alt | caption | other_names | hiero | Old_Norse | script_name | script | affiliation | cult_center | abode | planet | mantra | weapon | battles | artifacts | animals | symbol | day | color | number | consort | parents | siblings | offspring | army | mount | texts | Greek_equivalent | Roman_equivalent | Etruscan_equivalent | Christian_equivalent | Slavic_equivalent | Hinduism_equivalent | Canaanite_equivalent| Indo-european_equivalent | Albanian_equivalent | Celtic_equivalent | equivalent1_type | equivalent1 | equivalent2_type | equivalent2 | equivalent3_type | equivalent3 | equivalent4_type | equivalent4 | equivalent5_type | equivalent5 | region | ethnic_group | festivals | height | age | tree | predecessor | successor | adherents | gender | venerated_in }} {{Infobox deity | type = | name = | deity_of = | member_of = | image = | alt = | caption = | other_names = | hiero = | avatar_birth = | avatar_end = | Old_Norse = | script_name = | script = | affiliation = | cult_center = | abode = | planet = | mantra = | weapon = | battles = | artifacts = | animals = | symbol = | adherents = | height = | age = | tree = | day = | color = | number = | consort = | parents = | siblings = | offspring = | predecessor = | successor = | army = | mount = | texts = | gender = | Greek_equivalent = | Roman_equivalent = | Etruscan_equivalent = | Christian_equivalent = | Norse_equivalent = | Slavic_equivalent = | Hinduism_equivalent = | Canaanite_equivalent = | Indo-european_equivalent = | Albanian_equivalent = | Celtic_equivalent = | Maya_equivalent = | Aztec_equivalent = | Manipuri_equivalent = | equivalent1_type = | equivalent1 = | equivalent2_type = | equivalent2 = | equivalent3_type = | equivalent3 = | equivalent4_type = | equivalent4 = | equivalent5_type = | equivalent5 = | region = | ethnic_group = | festivals = }} {{clear}} ==Examples== {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[Mouse]] | symbols = [[Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[Mouse]] | symbols = [[Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{clear}} ==TemplateData== {{Collapse top|title=TemplateData}} {{TemplateData header|noheader=1}} { "description": "", "params": { "name": { "label": "Name", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "Character name to display in top header (default = PAGENAME)." }, "image": { "label": "Image", "type": "wiki-file-name", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "Image of the character. \"File:\" and wikilinking is not required. i.e. use {{para|image|Example.png}}." }, "image_upright": { "label": "Image upright", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "The image size defaults to frameless (220px). The size should not be set to a value that would result in an image width greater than 270px. See [[#Calculating \"image_upright\"|help below]] for guidance on determining the value to be used." }, "alt": { "label": "Alt", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "Alt text for image per [[WP:MOSALT]]." }, "caption": { "label": "Caption", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "A caption explaining the image." }, "type": { "label": "Type", "description": "Religion in which the deity is worshipped", "example": "Hindu for Rama", "type": "string", "required": true }, "avatar_birth" : {}, "avatar_end": {}, "deity_of": {}, "god_of": {}, "alusi_of": {}, "member_of": {}, "qid": {}, "suppressfields": {}, "fetchwikidata": {}, "image_size": {}, "other_names": {}, revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{Lua|Module:InfoboxImage|Module:WikidataIB|Module:Check for unknown parameters}} {{Tracks Wikidata|P18|cat=No local image but image on Wikidata}} The template can be used for any god, deity or mythological figure who is worshiped in a religion or cult. For other mythological figures, please consider other templates like [[Template:Infobox mythical creature]] or [[Template:Infobox character]]. == All fields == {{Parameter names example | type | name | deity_of | member_of | image | alt | caption | other_names | hiero | Old_Norse | script_name | script | affiliation | cult_center | abode | planet | mantra | weapon | battles | artifacts | animals | symbol | day | color | number | consort | parents | siblings | offspring | army | mount | texts | Greek_equivalent | Roman_equivalent | Etruscan_equivalent | Christian_equivalent | Slavic_equivalent | Hinduism_equivalent | Canaanite_equivalent| Indo-european_equivalent | Albanian_equivalent | Celtic_equivalent | equivalent1_type | equivalent1 | equivalent2_type | equivalent2 | equivalent3_type | equivalent3 | equivalent4_type | equivalent4 | equivalent5_type | equivalent5 | region | ethnic_group | festivals | height | age | tree | predecessor | successor | adherents | gender | venerated_in }} {{Infobox deity | type = | name = | deity_of = | member_of = | image = | alt = | caption = | other_names = | hiero = | avatar_birth = | avatar_end = | Old_Norse = | script_name = | script = | affiliation = | cult_center = | abode = | planet = | mantra = | weapon = | battles = | artifacts = | animals = | symbol = | adherents = | height = | age = | tree = | day = | color = | number = | consort = | parents = | siblings = | offspring = | predecessor = | successor = | army = | mount = | texts = | gender = | Greek_equivalent = | Roman_equivalent = | Etruscan_equivalent = | Maya_equivalent = | Aztec_equivalent = | Manipuri_equivalent = | equivalent1_type = | equivalent1 = | equivalent2_type = | equivalent2 = | equivalent3_type = | equivalent3 = | equivalent4_type = | equivalent4 = | equivalent5_type = | equivalent5 = | region = | ethnic_group = | festivals = }} {{clear}} ==Examples== {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[Mouse]] | symbols = [[Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[Mouse]] | symbols = [[Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{clear}} ==TemplateData== {{Collapse top|title=TemplateData}} {{TemplateData header|noheader=1}} { "description": "", "params": { "name": { "label": "Name", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "Character name to display in top header (default = PAGENAME)." }, "image": { "label": "Image", "type": "wiki-file-name", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "Image of the character. \"File:\" and wikilinking is not required. i.e. use {{para|image|Example.png}}." }, "image_upright": { "label": "Image upright", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "The image size defaults to frameless (220px). {{Documentation subpage}} {{Lua|Module:InfoboxImage|Module:WikidataIB|Module:Check for unknown parameters}} {{Tracks Wikidata|P18|cat=No local image but image on Wikidata}} The template can be used for any god, deity or mythological figure who is worshiped in a religion or cult. For other mythological figures, please consider other templates like [[Template:Infobox mythical creature]] or [[Template:Infobox character]]. == All fields == {{Parameter names example | type | name equivalent4 = | equivalent5_type = | equivalent5 = | region = | ethnic_group = | festivals = }} {{clear}} ==Examples== {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[Mouse]] | symbols = [[Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ

In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ

{{Documentation subpage}} {{Lua|Module:InfoboxImage|Module:WikidataIB|Module:Check for unknown parameters}} {{Tracks Wikidata|P18|cat=No local image but image on Wikidata}} The template can be used for any god, deity or mythological figure who is worshiped in a religion or cult. For other mythological figures, please consider other templates like [[Template:Infobox mythical creature]] or [[Template:Infobox character]]. == All fields == {{Parameter names example | type | name | deity_of | member_of | image | alt | caption | other_names | hiero | Old_Norse | script_name | script | affiliation | cult_center | abode | planet | mantra | weapon | battles | artifacts | animals | symbol | day | color | number | consort | parents | siblings | offspring | army | mount | texts | Greek_equivalent | Roman_equivalent | Etruscan_equivalent | Christian_equivalent | Slavic_equivalent | Hinduism_equivalent | Canaanite_equivalent| Indo-european_equivalent | Albanian_equivalent | Celtic_equivalent | equivalent1_type | equivalent1 | equivalent2_type | equivalent2 | equivalent3_type | equivalent3 | equivalent4_type | equivalent4 | equivalent5_type | equivalent5 | region | ethnic_group | festivals | height | age | tree | predecessor | successor | adherents | gender | venerated_in }} {{Infobox deity | type = | name = | deity_of = | member_of = | image = | alt = | caption = | other_names = | hiero = | avatar_birth = | avatar_end = | Old_Norse = | script_name = | script = | equivalent4 = | equivalent5_type = | equivalent5 = | region = | ethnic_group = | festivals = }} {{clear}} ==Examples== {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[Mouse]] | symbols = [[Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ

p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,

[[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ

[[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,

[[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[Brahman]] ([[Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[:en:Buddhi|Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[Mouse]] | symbols = [[Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[:en:Brahman|Brahman]] ([[:en:Ganapatya|Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[:en:Buddhi|Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[Mouse]] | symbols = [[Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[:en:Ganesh Chaturthi|Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[:en:Kartikeya|Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[:en:National Museum, New Delhi|National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[:en:Brahman|Brahman]] ([[:en:Ganapatya|Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ

[[Parvati]] | siblings = [[:en:Kartikeya|Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[:en:National Museum, New Delhi|National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[:en:Brahman|Brahman]] ([[:en:Ganapatya|Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[:en:Buddhi|Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[Mouse]] | symbols = [[Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[:en:Ganesh Chaturthi|Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[:en:Kartikeya|Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[:en:Kartikeya|Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[:en:Basohli|Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[:en:National Museum, New Delhi|National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[:en:Brahman|Brahman]] ([[:en:Ganapatya|Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[:en:Mount Kailash|Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[:en:Buddhi|Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[Mouse]] | symbols = [[Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[:en:Ganesh Chaturthi|Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[:en:Kartikeya|Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[:en:Basohli|Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[:en:Brahman|Brahman]] ([[:en:Ganapatya|Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ

[[Parvati]] | siblings = [[:en:Kartikeya|Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[:en:Basohli|Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[:en:National Museum, New Delhi|National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[:en:Brahman|Brahman]] ([[:en:Ganapatya|Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[:en:Panchayatana puja|Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[:en:Mount Kailash|Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[:en:Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[:en:Buddhi|Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[:en:Mouse|Mouse]] | symbols = [[Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[:en:Ganesh Chaturthi|Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[Shiva]] | mother = [[Parvati]] | siblings = [[:en:Kartikeya|Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[:en:Basohli|Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. {{Documentation subpage}} {{Lua|Module:InfoboxImage|Module:WikidataIB|Module:Check for unknown parameters}} {{Tracks Wikidata|P18|cat=No local image but image on Wikidata}} The template can be used for any god, deity or mythological figure who is worshiped in a religion or cult. For other mythological figures, please consider other templates like [[Template:Infobox mythical creature]] or [[Template:Infobox character]]. == All fields == {{Parameter names example| abode | [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] | siblings = [[:en:Kartikeya|Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[:en:Basohli|Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[:en:National Museum, New Delhi|National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[:en:Brahman|Brahman]] ([[:en:Ganapatya|Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[:en:Panchayatana puja|Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[:en:Mount Kailash|Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[Shiva]] and [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]]) ,

circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[:en:Basohli|Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[:en:National Museum, New Delhi|National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[:en:Brahman|Brahman]] ([[:en:Ganapatya|Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[:en:Panchayatana puja|Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ

{{Documentation subpage}} {{Lua|Module:InfoboxImage|Module:WikidataIB|Module:Check for unknown parameters}} {{Tracks Wikidata|P18|cat=No local image but image on Wikidata}} The template can be used for any god, deity or mythological figure who is worshiped in a religion or cult. For other mythological figures, please consider other templates like [[Template:Infobox mythical creature]] or [[Template:Infobox character]]. == All fields == {{Parameter names example| abode | equivalent4_type = | equivalent4 = | equivalent5_type = | equivalent5 = }} {{clear}} ==Examples== {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[:en:Shiva|Shiva]] | mother = [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] | siblings = [[:en:Kartikeya|Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[:en:Basohli|Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[:en:National Museum, New Delhi|National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[:en:Brahman|Brahman]] ([[:en:Ganapatya|Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[:en:Panchayatana puja|Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[:en:Mount Kailash|Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[:en:Shiva|Shiva]] and [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[:en:Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[:en:Siddhi|Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[:en:Buddhi|Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[:en:Mouse|Mouse]] | symbols = [[:en:Swastika|Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[:en:Ganesh Chaturthi|Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[:en:Shiva|Shiva]] | mother = [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] | siblings = [[:en:Kartikeya|Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[:en:Basohli|Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[:en:National Museum, New Delhi|National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[:en:Brahman|Brahman]] ([[:en:Ganapatya|Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[:en:Panchayatana puja|Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[:en:Mount Kailash|Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[:en:Shiva|Shiva]] and [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[:en:Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[:en:Siddhi|Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[:en:Buddhi|Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[:en:Mouse|Mouse]] | symbols = [[:en:Swastika|Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[:en:Ganesh Chaturthi|Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{clear}} ==TemplateData== {{Collapse top|title=TemplateData}} {{TemplateData header|noheader=1}} { "description": "", "params": { "name": { "label": "Name", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "Character name to display in top header (default = PAGENAME)." }, "image": { "label": "Image", "type": "wiki-file-name", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "Image of the character. \"File:\" and wikilinking is not required. i.e. use {{para|image|Example.png}}." }, "image_upright": { "label": "Image upright", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "The image size defaults to frameless (220px). {{Documentation subpage}} {{Lua|Module:InfoboxImage|Module:WikidataIB|Module:Check for unknown parameters}} {{Tracks Wikidata|P18|cat=No local image but image on Wikidata}} The template can be used for any god, deity or mythological figure who is worshiped in a religion or cult. For other mythological figures, please consider other templates like [[Template:Infobox mythical creature]] or [[Template:Infobox character]]. == All fields == {{Parameter names example| abode | abodes | adherents | affiliation | age | alt | alusi_of | animals | army | artefacts | artifacts | associate | battles | birth_date | birth_name | birth_place | birthname | avatar_birth | avatar_end | spouse | successor | symbol | symbols | temple | temples | texts | tree | type | venerated_in | weapon | weapons | world }} {{Infobox deity | type = | name = | deity_of = | member_of = | image = | image_upright = | alt = | caption = | other_names = | hiero = | old_norse = | devanagari = | sanskrit_transliteration = | script_name = | script = | venerated_in = | associate = | cult_centre = | equivalent4_type = | equivalent4 = | equivalent5_type = | equivalent5 = }} {{clear}} ==Examples== {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[:en:Shiva|Shiva]] | mother = [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] | siblings = [[:en:Kartikeya|Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[:en:Basohli|Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[:en:National Museum, New Delhi|National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[:en:Brahman|Brahman]] ([[:en:Ganapatya|Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[:en:Panchayatana puja|Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[:en:Mount Kailash|Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[:en:Shiva|Shiva]] and [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[:en:Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[:en:Siddhi|Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[:en:Buddhi|Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[:en:Mouse|Mouse]] | symbols = [[:en:Swastika|Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[:en:Ganesh Chaturthi|Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{Infobox deity | type = Hindu | name = Ganesha | gender = Male | father = [[:en:Shiva|Shiva]] | mother = [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]] | siblings = [[:en:Kartikeya|Kartikeya]] | deity_of = {{ubl |God of New Beginnings, Success and Wisdom |Remover of Obstacles}} | image = Ganesha Basohli miniature circa 1730 Dubost p73.jpg | caption = [[:en:Basohli|Basohli]] miniature, c. 1730. [[:en:National Museum, New Delhi|National Museum, New Delhi]]. | alt = Attired in an orange dhoti, an elephant-headed man sits on a large lotus. His body is red in colour and he wears various golden necklaces and bracelets and a snake around his neck. On the three points of his crown, budding lotuses have been fixed. He holds in his two right hands the rosary (lower hand) and a cup filled with three modakas (round yellow sweets), a fourth modaka held by the curving trunk is just about to be tasted. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[:en:Brahman|Brahman]] ([[:en:Ganapatya|Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[:en:Panchayatana puja|Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
Oṃ Gaṃ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ}} | abode =• '''[[:en:Mount Kailash|Mount Kailash]]''' (with parents [[:en:Shiva|Shiva]] and [[:en:Parvati|Parvati]]) ,
• '''Ganeshloka''' | weapon = [[:en:Parashu|{{IAST|Paraśu}} (axe)]], [[Pasha (Hinduism)|{{IAST|pāśa}} (noose)]], [[Elephant goad|{{IAST|aṅkuśa}} (elephant goad)]] | consorts = {{ubl | [[Riddhi]] (Prosperity) | [[:en:Siddhi|Siddhi]] (Attainment) | [[:en:Buddhi|Buddhi]] (Intelligence) }} | mount = [[:en:Mouse|Mouse]] | symbols = [[:en:Swastika|Swastika]], [[Om]], [[Modak]] | festivals = [[:en:Ganesh Chaturthi|Ganesh Chaturthi]] | texts = ''[[Ganesha Purana]]'', ''[[Mudgala Purana]]'', ''[[Ganapati Atharvashirsa]]'' | children = Shubha, Labha, [[Santoshi Mata]] }} {{clear}} ==TemplateData== {{Collapse top|title=TemplateData}} {{TemplateData header|noheader=1}} { "description": "", "params": { "name": { "label": "Name", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "Character name to display in top header (default = PAGENAME)." }, "image": { "label": "Image", "type": "wiki-file-name", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "Image of the character. \"File:\" and wikilinking is not required. i.e. use {{para|image|Example.png}}." }, "image_upright": { "label": "Image upright", "type": "string", "required": false, "suggested": false, "deprecated": false, "description": "The image size defaults to frameless (220px). The size should not be set to a value that would result in an image width greater than 270px. In his two left hands, he holds a lotus in the upper hand and an axe in the lower one, with its handle leaning against his shoulder. | affiliation = [[:en:Deva (Hinduism)|Deva]], [[:en:Brahman|Brahman]] ([[:en:Ganapatya|Ganapatya]]), [[Saguna Brahman]] ([[:en:Panchayatana puja|Panchayatana puja]]) | mantra = {{IAST|Oṃ Shri Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ
